options.why: Stopped at $a^2=225$ without taking the square root.
Subtracted the lengths ($17-8$) instead of their squares.
Added the squares ($100+16$) instead of subtracting them.
Left the answer as $a^2=84$ without taking the square root.
Subtracted the lengths ($10-4$) instead of their squares.
Did not take the square root of $40$.
Added the squares ($49+9$) instead of subtracting them.
Rounded the third decimal up when it should round down.

options.text: He added the squares; since $13$ is the hypotenuse he must subtract, so $a=\sqrt{169-25}=12\text{ cm}$.
He forgot to take the square root.
options.why: He did take a square root, giving $\sqrt{194}$; the error is adding rather than subtracting.

options.text: He used the wrong side as the hypotenuse.
options.why: He correctly treated $13$ as the hypotenuse; his error was adding the squares.

options.text: He should have divided the squares.
options.why: Pythagoras never divides the squares; a shorter side needs subtraction.
